summary: Multi-character fighter matching PR touching Crazy Hand, Kirby, Popo/Nana, and related item headers. Bot report records +7136 matched code bytes, 20 new matched functions, 8 unmatched-item improvements, and 2 .sdata2 regressions. No PR body or human review comments were present in the slice.

key_files.role: Largest code change; replaces several nonmatching markers with Crazy Hand init/wait/bomb/grab functions, adds helper struct for ftCh_Init_803D4878 data, adds includes, and restructures table data.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCh_Init_80156198, ftCh_Init_80156310, ftCh_Wait1_0_Anim, ftCh_Init_80156AD8, fn_801588B8, fn_8015B548
bot_report: CrazyHand new matches and ftCh_Init_80156AD8 improvement

key_files.role: Corrects several Crazy Hand attribute fields from s32 to float, aligning header types with float position/offset arithmetic in ftCh_Init.c.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCrazyHandAttributes x20, x6C, x70, x84, x90, x94, xA0, xB0, xB4 changed to float

key_files.role: Header updates corresponding to recovered Kirby init/helper signatures.
Adds several Kirby/Yoshi egg capture animation functions and improves Peach/Fire Emblem/Marth copy-special functions.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_YsSpecialAirNCapture1_Anim, ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture1_Anim, ftKb_YsSpecialAirCapture2_Anim, ftKb_YsSpecialAirCapture1_Anim, ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim
bot_report: five Kirby/Yoshi egg functions newly matched; ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 and ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C06C improved to 99.82%

key_files.role: Small Nana-side changes affecting Popo/Nana special-S collision scores.
evidence_refs: bot_report: ftPp_SpecialS_1_Coll and ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll small improvements in Nana unit

key_files.role: Large Popo special-S/special-Hi/special-Lw matching work with many helper inlines and PAD_STACK adjustments.
evidence_refs: diff: ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0, ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow_0_Phys, ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow2_Phys, ftPp_SpecialAirHiThrow2_Coll, ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll, fn_80122D2C
bot_report: multiple Popo new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Adjusts Popo type padding/fields around offsets used by the special move work.
evidence_refs: diff: Popo type padding changes near _7C, _9C, _B8

key_files.role: Adds item-side struct declarations for climbers string attributes.
Adds declarations/includes for climbers string item integration.

what_changed: Replaced multiple Crazy Hand nonmatching markers with C implementations, including ftCh_Wait1_0_Anim, ftCh_Init_80156198, ftCh_Init_80156310, fn_801588B8, and fn_8015B548.
Introduced a typed ftCh_Init_803D4878_t structure with states, x48, u8_pair x5C, and x68 arrays, replacing an anonymous enum/byte blob initializer for Crazy Hand state selection data.
Corrected several ftCrazyHandAttributes fields from s32 to float where the new code uses them as positions, offsets, or random float ranges.
Implemented Kirby hat and Yoshi egg related functions across ftKb_Init.c and ftKb_SpecialNYs.c, including several newly matched capture animation functions.
Implemented or improved Popo special-S, special-Hi, and special-Lw functions, using helper inlines, collision/physics helpers, and stack padding adjustments.
Added item header declarations for climbers string attributes and related item integration.
Bot report recorded 20 new matches, 8 unmatched-item improvements, and 2 unmatched .sdata2 regressions.

smart_moves.text: Header type corrections were made alongside code recovery: Crazy Hand attributes used in float arithmetic were changed from s32 to float instead of compensating locally in function bodies.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCh_Init.c uses da fields in Vec3 float positions and random range arithmetic

smart_moves.text: The Crazy Hand state-selection data was decomposed into a named local struct layout rather than leaving a flat anonymous byte array, making x48, x5C pairs, and x68 index data separately reviewable.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCh_Init_803D4878 initializer split into states, x48, x5C, x68

smart_moves.text: Repeated Crazy Hand motion dispatch was expressed as switch cases over ftMh_MS_* identifiers, which is more searchable and reviewable than address-only dispatch.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCh_Wait1_0_Anim switch over ftMh_MS_Entry through ftMh_MS_Slam

smart_moves.text: Kirby copy-special branch ladders for Marth/Fire Emblem end motion IDs were simplified into ternary assignments while preserving the two cmd_vars cases.
evidence_refs: diff: ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90 and ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C06C ternary msid selection

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering fighter code, update the owning fighter attribute header if the body proves fields are floats; the Crazy Hand body used these fields in Vec3 coordinate and random-range float expressions, and the PR changed the header accordingly.
evidence_refs: diff: ftCh_Init.c Vec3 and HSD_Randf arithmetic using da->x20/x6C/x70/x84/x90/x94/xA0/xB0/xB4

decomp_lessons.lesson: A flat data blob can sometimes be made reviewable by recovering a small typed table structure, as with Crazy Hand's state table split into state IDs, byte indices, u8_pair ranges, and transition data.

Reusable helper inlines can reduce duplicated matching logic in fighter move code, but this PR also shows that stack padding and inline-shaping tactics should be tracked as matching tactics rather than general style.
evidence_refs: diff: Popo helper inlines checkNanaInRange, incrementMvAndCheck, doFallPhys, ftPp_set_cbs
diff: PAD_STACK uses in Kirby and Popo

naming_conventions.convention: Unproven functions retained address-style names such as fn_801588B8, fn_8015B548, and fn_80122D2C rather than inventing semantic names.
Clear local roles use short conventional names such as fp, fp2, gobj, pos, prev, da, and vec.
Recovered table types may use symbol-derived names when semantics are not fully known, as with ftCh_Init_803D4878_t and offset-like fields x48/x5C/x68.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used in several Kirby and Popo functions, including ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010BF90, ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C06C, ftPp_SpecialS_80120FE0, and Popo throw/collision helpers.
evidence_refs: bot_report: affected functions newly matched or improved

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A scoped #pragma dont_inline on/reset was added around ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim.
Temporary local padding arrays appear in Crazy Hand and Popo code to influence stack layout, including u8 _[4], u8 __[20], and u8 _[16].
evidence_refs: diff: ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll_inline3 and ftPp_SpecialS_1_Coll_inline3 local u8 arrays

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Ternary expressions were used for motion-ID selection in Kirby copy-special functions, replacing nested if/else ladders.
Small helper inlines were introduced for repeated move logic, such as Crazy Hand button checks and Popo fall/callback helpers.

review_feedback.feedback: Progress report for GALE01 recorded matched code 60.76%, +0.18%, +7136 bytes.

20 new matches were reported, led by CrazyHand ftCh_Wait1_0_Anim (+1644), fn_801588B8 (+644), Popo fn_80122D2C (+432), CrazyHand ftCh_Init_80156198 (+376), Kirby ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Anim (+332), and Kirby ftKb_UnkIntBoolFunc0 (+320).
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new matches table

review_feedback.feedback: 8 unmatched-item improvements were reported, including CrazyHand ftCh_Init_80156AD8 to 98.86%, Popo start/collision functions, Kirby Peach copy-special functions, and small Nana unit improvements.
2 unmatched-item regressions were reported: Popo ftPp_SpecialS .sdata2 -2 bytes and CrazyHand ftCh_Init .sdata2 -2 bytes.
No human review comments or PR body rationale were available.

follow_up_queries: ftCh_Init_803D4878_t u8_pair x5C x68 CrazyHand state table
ftCrazyHandAttributes s32 float x20 x6C x70 x84 x90 x94 xA0 xB0 xB4

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, merge state, changed files, counts
raw_diff/diff_excerpt: concrete code changes, but loaded diff was truncated in prompt
text_corpus: bot report with match/improvement/regression tables
metadata_json: no review comments or reviews
notes: Evidence is strong for changed files and bot-reported matching results.
Evidence is weaker for author intent because the PR body is empty and there are no human review comments.
Loaded diff excerpt was truncated, so detailed claims are limited to visible hunks and targeted local diff lookup.
Bot report supports match/improvement/regression outcomes, but no separate build logs or objdiff artifacts were provided beyond the bot summary.
PAD_STACK, local padding arrays, and #pragma dont_inline are recorded as tactics used in this PR, not promoted as general style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Type fields in headers when recovered bodies prove float usage
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2359 pairs Crazy Hand function recovery with ftCrazyHandAttributes field corrections from s32 to float where the body uses those fields for positions, offsets, and random float ranges.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover small typed data-table layouts instead of leaving byte blobs
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2359 replaces an anonymous Crazy Hand state byte blob with ftCh_Init_803D4878_t and u8_pair ranges, making the table's state IDs and index/range sections explicit.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Track PAD_STACK and dont_inline as evidence-dependent tactics
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2359 uses PAD_STACK and a scoped #pragma dont_inline around functions that the bot reports as matched or improved; these are useful search examples but should remain evidence-dependent tactics, not broad source style.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all PAD_STACK or pragma uses are acceptable from this PR; loaded standards require narrow verifier evidence and scoped exceptions.
Do not infer semantic names for fn_801588B8, fn_8015B548, or fn_80122D2C; the PR retained address-style names.
Do not treat the .sdata2 regressions as acceptable or resolved beyond the bot report noting them; no human rationale was present.
Do not promote a global rule that gobj->user_data should replace GET_FIGHTER; the diff itself marks some gobj->user_data uses with TODO #GET_FIGHTER.
